steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Distribute $(x^{-9}y^{6}z^{5})$ across each term of $(x^{4}z^{5}+y^{8}z^{-7})$ by adding exponents on matching bases.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$x^{-5}y^{6}z^{10}+y^{8}z^{-7}$': the second term drops the $x^{-9}$ and the $y^6$ factors from the monomial — incomplete distribution. ✗
  • Choice B — '$x^{-5}y^{6}z^{10}+x^{-9}y^{14}z^{-2}$': first term $x^{-9+4}y^{6}z^{5+5}=x^{-5}y^{6}z^{10}$ ✓; second term $x^{-9}y^{6+8}z^{5+(-7)}=x^{-9}y^{14}z^{-2}$ ✓. Both correct. ✓
  • Choice C — '$x^{-5}z^{10}+x^{-9}z^{-2}$': drops all $y$ factors from both terms. ✗
  • Choice D — '$x^{-5}z^{10}+x^{14}z^{-2}$': drops $y$ factors and adds exponents incorrectly for the second term ($-9+8=14$?). ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
